model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Vanderbilt is the safer pick because the underlying team-quality gap is meaningful: the Commodores rate around No. 10-12 nationally in the major predictive systems and bring an elite offense into this matchup, while McNeese profiles more like a solid but clearly lower-tier tournament team. McNeese has a real 12-over-5 upset path if it slows the game and creates shooting variance, which keeps confidence below the top tier. Still, Vanderbilt’s SEC-tested résumé, stronger two-way efficiency, and recent form make it the more reliable advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced team-quality edge favors Vanderbilt over a live underdog
reasoningStep.summary: Vanderbilt has the stronger predictive profile, better schedule-tested résumé, and a major offensive edge, while McNeese is good enough to make this a credible upset spot but not enough to outweigh the overall gap.
evidence: Vanderbilt ratings: Torvik No. 10 and KenPom No. 12, with an adjusted offense around No. 6 nationally and defense around top 30.
McNeese ratings: KenPom No. 67, with adjusted offense around No. 91 and adjusted defense around 101.8, indicating a clear full-season efficiency gap versus Vanderbilt.
Recent context: Vanderbilt reached the SEC tournament final and owns marquee wins over Alabama, Tennessee, and Florida, showing high-end competition readiness.
Upset-risk review: outside bracket analysis identified this as a plausible 5/12 upset matchup, so the underdog path is real and lowers confidence.
Availability check did not surface a major confirmed injury concern significant enough to change the pick.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://uwhuskieswire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/vanderbilt/2026/03/15/vanderbilt-mcneese-march-madness-bracket-ncaa-tournament-2026/89025983007/ and extracted 2155 characters of text.

result.text: Vanderbilt March Madness opponent is McNeese in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Big Ten Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Vanderbilt basketball’s March Madness opponent is McNeese to open 2026 NCAA tournament bracket Aria Gerson Nashville Tennessean Vanderbilt basketball was selected as a No. 5 seed in March Madness in the South Region, where it will face McNeese State in Oklahoma City to start the 2026 NCAA Tournament on March 19. The Commodores will tip off at approximately 2:15 p.m. CT on TruTV. If Vanderbilt advances, it would face the winner of No. 4 Nebraska vs No. 13 Troy. The No. 1 seed in this region is Florida. The Commodores (26-8) finished tied for fourth in the SEC with an 11-7 conference record. Their marquee wins included over Alabama at home, Tennessee on the road and Florida in the SEC tournament. Vanderbilt made it to the final of the SEC tournament on March 15 for the first time since 2012, but lost to Arkansas 86-75 at Bridgestone Arena . Buy Vanderbilt NCAA tournament tickets Vanderbilt is making its second straight NCAA tournament appearance after having not appeared since 2017. Last season, the 10-seed Commodores lost in their first game to Saint Mary's. Printable March Madness bracket March Madness bracket, printable 2026 NCAA tournament page for picks, predictions This season, Vanderbilt has improved in its second year under coach Mark Byington, with Tyler Tanner taking a step forward and becoming a first-team All-SEC player in his sophomore season. Tanner also was named to the SEC All-Defensive Team. Think you can beat our expert? Bracket Challenge Sweepstakes | USA TODAY Sports What time does Vanderbilt play McNeese in 2026 NCAA Tournament? Date: Thursday, March 19 Time: Approximately 2:15 p.m. CT TV: TruTV Aria Gerson covers Vanderbilt athletics for The Tennessean. Contact her at agerson@gannett.com or on X @aria_gerson . Bracket Busters NBA Prospects to Watch The 10 most likely men’s NCAA tournament upsets for March Madness 2026 Barry Evans and the VCU Rams could be March Madness Cinderellas once again. Mitchell Layton / Getty Images By Jordan Brenner and Peter Keating March 16, 2026 Updated March 17, 2026 4:03 pm EDT Share full article 25 The NCAA Tournament is all about upsets, and as doctors in this field of study, we’ve crunched the numbers and have assembled our annual list of likely threats and victims. McNeese, VCU and High Point: We’re sizing you up for glass slippers. UNC, Vanderbilt and Wisconsin? You might need some Rolaids. Advertisement Welcome to Year 20 of Bracket Breakers! If you’ve been following us this whole time, you know to expect a whole lot of upset-predicting math and even more puns in the coming days. If you’re new to the scene, here’s what to expect: We will provide deep, regional breakdowns of every first-round matchup with a difference of at least five seeds, powered by our upset-seeking model, Slingshot. You can read a detailed explanation of how the process works here . Want a closer look? Dig into our Definitive Guide to First-Round Upsets for in-depth explanations as to why our model projects this year’s upsets the way it does. But for now, here’s a quick look at the 10 most likely first-round upsets according to our model. No. 5 Vanderbilt Commodores vs. No. 12 McNeese Cowboys Upset Chance: 41.2 percent Last week, we identified the Cowboys as our model’s second-favorite potential Bracket Breaker . And since Santa Clara (our top choice) ended up seeded 10th, putting them in a game without a five-seed difference, McNeese now tops our chart. The Cowboys lead the country in forcing turnovers (24.9 percent of opponents’ possessions), and they grab offensive boards at a top-50 rate, which makes Slingshot positively swoon. Their upset odds would be even higher if not for the fact that Vandy profiles as an extremely safe favorite, or as we call them, a G

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
